name: Bumba
description: Bumba, also known as Mbombo, is the creator god in the mythology of the Bushongo people of Central Africa. According to their creation myth, in the beginning, Bumba was alone, enveloped by a primordial darkness and water. He suffered from a terrible stomach ache and, after a long period of suffering, vomited up the sun, moon, and stars. The heat of the sun caused the water to evaporate, revealing land. He then vomited up nine animals: the leopard Koy Bumba, the eagle Ponga Bumba, the crocodile Ganda Bumba, the fish Yo, the turtle Kono Bumba, a heron, the beetle Scarab, a goat Budi, and finally a man named Loko Yima. These animals, in turn, created many other things, populating the earth with plants, animals, and people. Bumba, however, was not satisfied with the noisy creation, so he created termites that burrowed underground, and from the earth emerged more humans. He then told Loko Yima to create death, which brought sorrow to the world. Bumba is often depicted as a benevolent but somewhat aloof creator, more concerned with the initial act of creation than with the ongoing affairs of the world. He represents the power of creation from nothingness and the origin of life and the universe itself. While he is not actively worshipped in the same way as some other deities, his story is central to the Bushongo worldview and their understanding of existence.
subtitle: The Creator God of the Bushongo

keyMyths.title: The Vomiting of Creation
keyMyths.description: Bumba, suffering from a stomach ache, vomited up the sun, moon, stars, and nine animals, which then populated the earth. This act marks the beginning of the Bushongo universe and the creation of all living things.
keyMyths.source: Torday and Joyce, Notes Ethnographiques sur les Peuples communément appelés Bakuba, ainsi que sur les Peuples apparentés. – Les Bushongo.

keyMyths.title: The Creation of Man
keyMyths.description: After the initial creation, Bumba was dissatisfied with the noise, so he created termites that burrowed underground, leading to the emergence of more humans. This second act of creation further populated the earth with humanity.
keyMyths.source: Vansina, Jan. The Children of Woot: A History of the Kuba Peoples.

keyMyths.title: The Introduction of Death
keyMyths.description: Bumba tasked Loko Yima with creating death, which brought sorrow into the world. This event explains the presence of suffering and mortality within the Bushongo worldview.
keyMyths.source: Frazer, James George. Folk-lore in the Old Testament: Studies in Comparative Religion, Legend and Law.

sources.author: Emil Torday and Thomas Athol Joyce
sources.work: Notes Ethnographiques sur les Peuples communément appelés Bakuba, ainsi que sur les Peuples apparentés. – Les Bushongo
